title: The Lost Pilot by Edward Taylor — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
for my father, 1922-1944

Your face did not rot
like the others--the co-pilot,
for example, I saw him

yesterday. His face is corn-
mush: his wife and daughter,
meaning: "The Lost Pilot" by Edward Taylor is a nature poem consisting of 65 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "for my father, 1922-1944 ...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
